dqc_rules.message: HIV screening coverage exceeds 200% of projected population
dqc_rules.hint: Coverage is measured against a projected population (an estimate), so over 100% can be real. Over 200% almost always means a data error — check the count or the population figure in the source file and re-upload.
Reactive cases are a subset of those screened, so this should never exceed 100%. Check the source file — likely a transcription error.

notes: Quarterly upload only (Qtr1-4). No Annual sheet ingestion — the template's Annual tab is a pure Excel rollup of the 4 quarters (confirmed via formula inspection); the dashboard computes the annual view from stored quarterly data instead of trusting the sheet's own formula. Column 1 (blank header, 'NIRA' abbreviation) is a redundant convenience column added later per the file's change_log — intentionally not mapped. No sex disaggregation (age-band only: 10-14/15-19/20-49) — this is an antenatal (pregnant-women) indicator, not a general-population one. HIV_REACTIVE_* columns are is_sensitive=True per CLAUDE.md's explicit sensitive-indicator policy — dashboard shows aggregated totals only for unauthorized roles.
